title: ranunculus spicatus subsp. rupestris
intro:
Ranunculus spicatus subsp. rupestris, also known as the rocky buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its yellow or white flowers and hairy leaves.

The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of alkaloids and saponins.

In terms of health benefits, the plant has been reported to exhibit antioxidant, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can help to protect against infections, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.

Ranunculus spicatus subsp. rupestris can be prepared in various ways, including as a tea, tincture, or infusion, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from skin conditions to respiratory problems.
